articles.title: The Abduction of Pocahontas Frontispiece
articles.notes: The provided PDF images show advertisements and the magazine cover/contents page, but do not contain the frontispiece image or any article about 'The Abduction of Pocahontas'. A frontispiece is typically an illustration facing the title page, which would not contain extractable text content.

articles.title: Mother's Evening Song
articles.author: Amy M. Rice
articles.notes: The poem 'Mother's Evening Song' by Amy M. Rice is visible in the PDF images and has already been extracted in the existing file March_Vol15_02_Mother'sEveningSong_Mother'sEveningSong.txt. It was the second poem to receive Honorable Mention in the Eliza Roxey Snow Poetry Contest.

articles.title: Zion's Mountains
articles.complete_text:
Zion's Mountains
By Nina Kerrick

Oh! Mountains! Oh! thou lofty peaks
How dear thou art to me,
Oh! let me dwell among thee here
As long as life shall be.

And then, Oh! lay me at thy feet
Beside some murmuring stream,
Where I can sleep, and hear thy voice,
In death's forgetful dream.

The rippling waves among the rocks,
Would sing to me I know,
Because methinks they all would know
That I had loved them so.

And then the wind would speak to me
While passing o'er my tomb,
And in the spring the flowers dear
Above my head would bloom.

Oh! no, I will not lonely be
If thou wilt let me dwell with thee
Oh, Mountains—
Through eternity.
articles.author: Nina Kerrick
articles.notes: Found complete poem on page 174 of the PDF images. It's a nature poem expressing love for mountains and desire to dwell among them in life and death.
